11: This chapter examines the crucial roles of learning and adaptation in Artificial
12: Intelligence. AI agents enhance their performance through continuous data
13: acquisition and experience. The Self-Improving Coding Agent (SICA) exemplifies this
14: by autonomously improving its capabilities through code modifications.
15: We have reviewed the fundamental components of agentic AI, including architecture,
16: applications, planning, multi-agent collaboration, memory management, and learning
17: and adaptation. Learning principles are particularly vital for coordinated improvement
18: in multi-agent systems. To achieve this, tuning data must accurately reflect the
19: complete interaction trajectory, capturing the individual inputs and outputs of each
21: These elements contribute to significant advancements, such as Google's
22: AlphaEvolve. This AI system independently discovers and refines algorithms by LLMs,
23: automated assessment, and an evolutionary approach, driving progress in scientific
24: research and computational techniques. Such patterns can be combined to construct
25: sophisticated AI systems. Developments like AlphaEvolve demonstrate that
26: autonomous algorithmic discovery and optimization by AI agents are attainable.
